entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Two Tenants with different FYV (4-4...
entry.content:
Hi,

let's distinguish in 

a) business / accounting

The fact that 2 different FYV should be used indicates that closing calendars might not be aligned cross-group which might lead to questions/issues during the closing process such as: which exchange rates should be applied? What is the cut-off date for intercompany balances between companies with different FYV? Does the auditor approve this setup (i.e. considers the inconsistencies immaterial)?

These are rather pure business / accounting related conceptional questions that should get answered.

b) technical

This depends on how the data submission should be done into GR. The most flexible way is leveraging GRDC (e.g. mapping to pull data from S/4 accounting tables) and associate source periods to GR target periods. E.g. if auditor agrees to it, the periods could get mapped 1:1.

The most restrictive option is to use the release task to source accounting (Group Reporting Preparation Ledger) from the same tenant/system instance where GR is installed. The system checks that the source FYV is consistent to the GR FYV maintained in the version (application help). This will exclude some scenarios.

Hope this helps.

Best,

Florian (PM Group Reporting)

entry.title: Unable to see the calls in CPI Monitoring page
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We have 1 flow in CPI which has successfully developed. Flow is APIM- SAP CPI- HR system. We were able to test the flow and we were able to see the calls in HR back end system also. But In CPI monitoring page, we were unable to see the calls. We were able to see the Http logs also. Attached screenshot.

entry.title: Powerdesigner: Comment obtenir les template excel de chargement de modèles ?
entry.content:
Bonjour,

Je cherche un template excel complet (un maximum d'attributs) afin d'utiliser la fonctionnalité d'import de l'extension ExcelImport.xem, pour un modèle physique et un modèle conceptuel. 

Merci beaucoup!

entry.title: LMS notifications
entry.content:
Hi!

I would like to investigate the source within the LMS from which certain parts of the notifications are being pulled.

The section below “You can access your learning plan via the following link:” does not appear to be part of the Email Notification Templates body. If this content is not included in the template itself, do you know where this additional text and the associated image (which currently is corrupted) might be managed and updated?

Notification screenshot:

Thank you, 

Anna

entry.title: Re: Restart Failed E-Signatures Without Restarting the Entire Onboarding Process post 1H 2026 Releas
entry.content:
@padma_123  this feature has not been introduced in the Production Environment due to some regressions. It is only available in Preview instance. 
You can follow this KBA for updates on when it would be released to Production Instances: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003754038

 

Regards,

Akarsh

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Why is Process Free Form Payments (F2564) called "Online Payment Flow" in Signavio? Wh
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: SAP Transportation Management ( TM) Reports for Logistics Integration
entry.content:
Reports for Logistics Integration

This overview lists reports available in the logistics integration area of Transportation Management (TM). These reports assist with integration and post-integration processes between TM and Sales and Distribution (SD), Materials Management (MM), Logistics Execution (LE), and nJIT (Just-In-Time).

1. Report to Check Integration for SO/PO/Delivery and FU

Technical Name: /SCMTMS/CHK_DOC_INT_TM_INTEGR

2. Activation of Purchase Scheduling Agreements Without Delivery Integration

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/ACTIVATE_MMSAG_WO_DLV
  • Description: Run to activate the integration of purchase scheduling agreements without integrating the corresponding inbound deliveries.

3. Activation of Documents for Transportation Management

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/LI_ACTIVATE
  • Description: Run to activate the integration of business transaction documents into TM in an internal integration setup.

4. Adjustment of Integration of Purchase Scheduling Agreements Without Delivery Integration

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/ADAPT_START_MM_WO_DLV
  • Description: Run to adjust the integration start date and reduction quantity for a purchase scheduling agreement item without delivery integration.

5. Create Deliveries in ERP

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/DLV_BATCH
  • Description: Run to create deliveries based on existing freight units.

6. Deactivation of Purchase Scheduling Agreements

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/DEACTIVATE_MMSAG_W_DLV
  • Description: Run to deactivate your purchase scheduling agreements.

7. Prepone End Dates for Items in Purchase Scheduling Agreement

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/UPDATE_INT_TIMERANGE
  • Description: Run to verify end dates and reschedule them for an earlier date.

8. Reactivation of Purchase Scheduling Agreements

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/REACTIVATE_MMSAG_W_DLV
  • Description: Run to reactivate the integration of purchase scheduling agreements into TM.

9. Setting and Fixing of Integration Time Range End Date for Purchase Scheduling Agreements

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/FIX_INT_TIMERANGE
  • Description: Run to set a fixed end date for the end of the integration of purchase scheduling agreements.

10. Subsequent Freight Unit Building in Internal TM Component Integration

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/SUBSEQENT_FUB_LOGINT
  • Description: Run to trigger the creation of freight unit in an internal TM component integration setup.

11. Update of Purchase Scheduling Agreements Based on TM Planning Results

  • Technical Name: /SCMTMS/TM_SAG_PLANNING_SYNC
  • Description: Run to synchronize the transportation planning date of freight unit items with the delivery date of the corresponding schedule line in the purchase scheduling agreement.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse/ ADT very slow
entry.content:
Do you mean this part: General - Startup and Shutdown?

I deactivated all SAP HANA Studio Stuff and I also checked "Skip exclusion check on startup", but it didn't change anything. 😪

entry.title: From One Product to Infinite Possibilities: What AVC Can Actually Do in the Public Cloud
entry.content:
If you have only encountered AVC in passing, you might be wondering: what exactly can it handle in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition? That is the question I want to answer today — not with a feature list, but with a look at the real business processes it supports.

It starts with a model, not a product

The foundational idea behind AVC is deceptively simple: instead of maintaining a separate master record for every possible product variation, you maintain one configurable product model that captures all the rules, dependencies, and pricing logic. A bicycle manufacturer does not need two thousand SKUs for every combination of frame size, colour, gear system, and accessory package. They need one model — and AVC handles the rest.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, this approach is supported across a wide range of business processes. Let me walk through each of them.

Make-to-Order: where AVC earns its reputation

The scenario most closely associated with AVC is Make-to-Order production with variant configuration. A customer selects their configuration in a sales order. AVC validates it against the characteristic values and dependencies defined in the model, generates the correct bill of materials and routing, and passes that directly into production. No manual translation. No re-entry of requirements. What the customer ordered is exactly what manufacturing builds.

This is the core process for industrial machinery, specialty equipment, and any product where the customer's specification and the production order must match without interpretation in between. In SAP scope terms, this is the LS1 scenario — and for many companies, it is the reason they implemented AVC in the first place.

Make-to-Stock: configuration without a customer order

Not every variant business runs on individual customer orders. If you produce configurable products in advance — for example, pre-configured machines at standard option levels that you know will sell — AVC supports this through material variants. You define a specific combination of characteristics as a material variant, plan and produce against it, and sell from stock when the order arrives.

This approach works well where lead times need to be short, where demand patterns are predictable, or where a small set of popular configurations covers the majority of your sales volume. It gives you the modelling power of AVC without the wait times of pure Make-to-Order.

Engineer-to-Order: built for unique, complex orders

Engineer-to-Order with AVC is the right fit for capital equipment, infrastructure components, or any scenario where each order is genuinely unique and requires dedicated oversight. The configuration becomes the foundation for everything that follows — from procurement to delivery.

Two more processes worth knowing

AVC in the Public Edition does not stop at the two core scenarios. There are two additional processes that extend its reach further.

Solution bundling allows you to combine multiple configurable products into a single customer offer. When a customer is configuring a system made up of several independently-configurable components — a production line, a modular installation, a hardware and software bundle — solution bundling lets you manage the whole as one coherent offer rather than a collection of separate line items.

Advanced intercompany sales processing supports AVC configurations that span company codes. If a configuration entered in one legal entity needs to flow through the intercompany supply chain to a delivering entity in a different country, AVC ensures the configuration is carried correctly across that boundary — which matters both for accuracy and for pricing.

Analytics that speak the language of configuration

Configurable products generate rich operational data — which characteristic combinations are most commonly selected, which options drive the longest lead times, where production deviations occur most often. AVC in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition includes embedded analytics that surface configuration-relevant KPIs, giving product managers and operations teams the visibility they need without building custom reports from scratch.

This is one of the areas where the public cloud delivery model makes a real difference. Reporting capabilities are part of the standard scope, updated with each release, and available to every customer on the platform.

What is in scope — and what is coming

Across all these processes, AVC in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition is supported by fifteen dedicated scope items — a number that has grown steadily as SAP has continued to invest in this area with each release. The roadmap points toward further AI-driven enhancements and expanded integration with adjacent processes, and we will cover those developments in a future post in this series.

If your business deals in products that are configured rather than simply selected, AVC is worth a careful look. The question is not whether your use case is supported — the chances are good that it is. The question is where to start.

For that, I would point you to the SAP Help Portal for AVC and to the first post in this series, which covers the conceptual foundations that make all of this possible.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Stages on Freight Order TM
entry.content: Are you talking about Freight Order based Road scenario or Freight Booking based Scenario?

entry.title: Re: Sizing the SAP Datasphere Object Store
entry.content:
Hello,

By below statments , do you mean HDLF compresses lesser than HANA?

1 GB CSV (Raw) -> ~1/8 GB in HANA

1 GB HANA  -> 1.35 GB in delta tables

 

Regards,

Viren

entry.title: Re: Batch determination by vendor number for the producti...
entry.content: any news here?

entry.title: Re: Change of Delivery Class of existing Database tables...
entry.content:
Hello @manichand_ale ,

Fiori Elements offers a copy&paste from spreadsheet application functionality. This can also be used in the Custom Business Configurations app: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-additional-functionality.html#b2a6ef2c-c563-4780-8dd9-dd44359778e0

In the ABAP Platform roadmap there was an RAP entry for 2027: File upload / Download Support via Static Actions
https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cross-product/roadmap-info/ui-services

However, it is not visible anymore. Maybe it was postponed. But the general idea is that the developer of the RAP BO can add an annotation in the behavior definition to enable a generic file upload action in the app. This way you would get a generic file upload in the Custom Business Configurations app.

The standalone Upload app F4638 is only released for SAP BTP.
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/upload-business-configuration#prerequisites
There is no entry in the Fiori library so it is not officially supported in S/4HANA.
https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/home

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Unsubscription failed
entry.content: HI @abdurraufib have you found the solution to this issue. I am encountering the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Change of Delivery Class of existing Database tables...
entry.content:
Hello @manichand_ale 


Changing delivery class from A → C is generally fine if the table stores configuration/customizing data and is needed for BCMO/Clean Core.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

FXN

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI2025/2027 Support for Apache To...
entry.content: @Giriraj_Harapanahalli, how about Tomcat 10?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation API - job error logs
entry.content:
Hello 


The jobId from Workflow/Monitoring is usually an Automation Job ID, not the runtime job ID expected by GET /v1/jobs/{jobId}


Use the monitoring/log APIs instead of /v1/jobs/{jobId}, expired jobs are automatically cleaned up, so old job IDs become invalid.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Sizing the SAP Datasphere Object Store
entry.content:
Hi @viren_devib35904 , 

"By below statments , do you mean HDLF compresses lesser than HANA?"

The 1,35 ratio is the average result of our tests, it will differ for each real table. This to to provide an assumption for sizing, when there are no measurements available.  

One thing we have seen is that delta tables are better at handling compound key, the indexes in HANA let the HANA Table use more resources for compound key.

entry.title: Re: SARA Retrieve
entry.content:
Hello @BE_JF 

to reload the archived data back into the database table the archiving object must have implemented a reload program. This is not the case for all SAP archiving objects. Reloading should only be used in exceptional cases.

In your case I would recommend to contact the Data Management and Landscape Transformation team, see also https://me.sap.com/notes/460620

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Automate BPEM Case Resolution Using Standard Configuration
entry.content: @Preeti3009  I am hoping your issue resolve now. pls suggest what approach work well for you.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI performance recommendations for Citrix-based...
entry.content:
Hello Robert, 

Although the note is quite old, the recommendations still apply to all subsequent and current SAP GUI versions. 

There is a best-practice guide attached to the note Troubleshooting Citrix Metaframe / SAP GUI Issues 

Some registry entries may need to take into account 64bit versions of SAP GUI. In this case, refer to the Administration guide in the SAP GUI DOCU folder.

Anna

entry.title: Re: SARA Retrieve
entry.content:
Have you already executed any "Reload" actions via the SARA transaction in some sandbox system?

 

Note:

  • Not all objects support this functionality.
  • Relevant OSS Notes may apply to specific objects.
  • Be aware of dependencies between objects before proceeding.

entry.title: Setting up Python in SAP Business Application Studio to trigger HANA Cloud Machine Learning
entry.content:
Are you looking for a place to run Python and Jupyter notebooks in an SAP environment? SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) might be the answer.

This blog shows how to set up a Python notebook in BAS, specifically for triggering the Machine Learning in SAP HANA Cloud or SAP Datasphere. That setup could be useful for an AI/ML expert who is creating and saving a prediction for business users to benefit from. Those business users could receive the insight for example in SAP Analytics Cloud, or wherever they need that information.

Thanks go to our Developer Advocate @Vitaliy-R  who introduced me to these steps in SAP Business Application Studio! 

Agenda

  • Architecture
  • Prerequisites
  • Setting up Python and Jupyter in SAP Business Application Studio
  • Configuring SAP HANA Cloud
  • Test connecting from a Jupyter Notebook to SAP HANA Cloud
  • What's next?

 

 

Architecture

The architecture for this example is as straightforward as it can be. Your data is in SAP HANA Cloud, where we also have Machine Learning algorithms (Predictive Analysis Library and Automated Predictive Library). We then use Python in a Jupyter Notebook running in SAP Business Application Studio to trigger the Machine Learning algorithms in SAP HANA Cloud. This is possible with SAP's hana_ml Python package.

This blog focuses mostly on a stand-alone SAP HANA Cloud. However, you can also trigger the same Machine Learning in SAP Datasphere's embedded SAP HANA Cloud. This blog has more details. 

More components are typically needed for a productive environment. To automate the creation of predictions the Python code can be schedules for instance with Cloud Foundry or SAP AI Core.

 

Prerequisites

This blog assumes that:

  • You have SAP Business Application Studio up and running
  • You have access to a SAP HANA Cloud instance, which has the Script Server activated

 

Setting up Python and Jupyter in SAP Business Application Studio

Go into the SAP Business Application Studio, for instance via the SAP Build Lobby. Create a new Dev space, name it "hana_ml". Chose the application kind "Basic" and in "Additional SAP Extensions" tick "Python Tools". Click "Create Dev Space".

 

After a short while the space will switch to "RUNNING". Click on the space to open it. You are now in the development environment of the SAP Business Application Studio. In the menu on the left open the “Extensions” section and “OK” an initial message that might pop up. You can then see that the extensions for both “Python” as well as “Jupyter” are already enabled / available. There is no “install” button available for those for instance, which means that they are already installed.

 

Now create a permanent Python environment into which you can install additional Python packages that will remain in place after restarting the Dev Space. Without these steps, any package installations would need to be reinstalled after a restart.

  • Open the Terminal with "View" → "Terminal".
  • You can check which Python version is being used: python --version
  • Create a folder for the environment with this command: mkdir ~/projects/hanaml
  • Go into the newly created folder: cd ~/projects/hanaml
  • Create a new Python environment called hanaml_env: python3 -m venv hanaml_env --upgrade-deps
  • Activate this new environment: source hanaml_env/bin/activate
  • Install SAP's Python package hana_ml: pip install hana_ml

 

With the following steps you open a Jupyter Notebook that uses the new Python environment:

  • IMPORTANT: Before opening a Jupyter Notebook you need to select the folder, in which your custom Python environment is installed. Click on “Explorer” in the menu in the left. Click “Open Folder”, then enter the path to the folder, which contains the custom environment, ie “home/user/projects/hanaml”.
  • To create and open a Jupyter Notebook go to the search box at the top of the page and select “Show and Run Commands”.
  • Type Jupyter into the search box and click “Create: New Jupyter Notebook”. A Jupyter Notebook opens up.
  • It is not yet using the Python environment that you created earlier. To switch to that new environment, click on “Select Kernel” on the top right hand side. Choose “Python environments…”. Then select the hanaml_env environment, which includes the hana_ml Package. Now the top right hand side of the Notebook shows the name of that kernel.

 

To verify that the hana_ml package is indeed available, run these two lines of code, to output the version of that package.

import hana_ml
print(hana_ml.__version__)

You should see a version like "2.28.26042901", whatever the current version is.

 

To save the Notebook, just use the menu on the left: File → Save.

 

Configuring SAP HANA Cloud

Now let's look at SAP HANA Cloud so that we can connect to it from the Jupyter Notebook in the SAP Business Application Studio. You can use an existing SAP HANA Cloud instance. It just needs to be configured and sized appropriately (see below). When creating a new instance on HANA Cloud Central, select these options:

  • For full control over the specifications select the option "Configure Manually":
  • Select a Memory of at least 48 GB. That is the minimum for the Script Server.
  • Select "Specific IP addresses and IP ranges (in addition to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP)". In most cases this will allow the SAP Business Application Studio to connect. If you would like to add BAS's external IP addresses on the allow list, you find these IP addresses in the documentation.
  • Under Additional Features select "Script Server".

Once SAP HANA Cloud is running, create a database user for Python to connect from BAS to SAP HANA Cloud. Open the SAP HANA Database Explorer (or your preferred SQL interface) and logon with the DBADMIN user. Create the user with these statements, which assign the required roles.

CREATE USER AIUSER PASSWORD YOURSECRETPASSWORD NO FORCE_FIRST_PASSWORD_CHANGE;
ALTER USER AIUSER DISABLE PASSWORD LIFETIME;
GRANT AFL__SYS_AFL_AFLPAL_EXECUTE TO AIUSER;
GRANT AFLPM_CREATOR_ERASER_EXECUTE to AIUSER;

 

Test connecting from a Jupyter Notebook to SAP HANA Cloud

One more thing, we need the endpoint of the SAP HANA Cloud instance to create a connection. Copy this from HANA Cloud Central.

 

Everything is in place to establish a connection from the Jupyter Notebook in the SAP Business Application Studio to SAP HANA Cloud. Enter your logon credentials in this code. The SQL endpoint needs to be passed as address parameter, but you have to remove the port from the string (":443"). The port is passed as separate parameter, but for SAP HANA Cloud this is always 443.

import hana_ml.dataframe as dataframe
conn = dataframe.ConnectionContext(address='YOURENDPOINT',
                                   port=443, 
                                   user='AIUSER', 
                                   password='YOURSECRETPASSWORD' 
                                   )
conn.connection.isconnected()

 

The isconnected() method should return "True". 

 

What's next?

This blog showed how to set up Python in SAP Business Application Studio to trigger HANA Cloud Machine Learning. Now you may want to actually trigger some Machine Learning in SAP HANA Cloud. 

A separate blog for the Machine Learning is on the way. Most likely it is going to be an updated version of the "Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud". If you don't want to wait, you can already get started with that example.

entry.title: SAP DRC strategy: ViDA readiness and global e-invoicing and e-reporting rollout
entry.content:
Our company runs primarily on US GAAP with limited local statutory ledger setup (no country version available) in many countries. Does that create gaps for DRC?

For example, missing local tax codes, document types or master data that the country reports require?  

How does a DRC implementation actually unfold in a country like France, E2E?

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to mass generate order confirmations?
entry.content: Thank you both for your input. I've come to realize that apparently I'm working with an incomplete version of SAP and limited permissions. Either the transactions suggested are not available or I can't access them. I will take the comments received here and open a ticket with our support to see if they can provide a way to do this. Thanks again everyone.

entry.title: Latest Home Page Onboarding External User
entry.content:
Hi everybody, 

the questions are regarding the latest home page in SAP Successfactors.

Is it possible to disable Quick Actions only for external Onboarding Users? If not, what could be a suggested alternative to hide Quick Actions for this particular external group? 

In another way, when trying to let the Quick Action enabled and choose the configured use case under "Manage My Data" the Quick Action is blank. 

Does anyone has an idea on this case?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: E-Mudhra integration with SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hello! Varun, replied on your email.

entry.title: Re: Information Design Tool does not connect to server 4....
entry.content: Thanks for sharing. No allowed list for thick clients. Updated your solution in the KBA error suggests. https://me.sap.com/notes/2151044

entry.title: SAP runs SAP BDC - Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data products
entry.content:
Discover how SAP is unleashing both human and artificial intelligence by leveraging data products in SAP Business Data Cloud. You will learn how we make the flywheel of AI, Data and Apps spin internally and how we turn SAP BDC into tangible offerings for the entire company. Through real examples, we will show how you can operationalize data products to accelerate insights, empower teams, and enable AI-driven innovation at scale with the benefits of SAP-Managed Data Products & Intelligent Applications while erasing IT as a bottleneck.

Usually we run these sessions live, but this time we decided to prerecord the presentation because our expert is expecting a very exciting family event around this time, and we didn’t want to risk canceling the session altogether.

So while the session is prerecorded, we’ll still be active in the chat and would love to hear your questions and thoughts throughout the session and afterwards.

Registration: https://events.sap.com/sap-runs-sap/en_us/home.html

Premier YouTube link: https://youtu.be/MizQtovd5FM (see above why this session is not run Live)

Session expert: Oliver Huth, Head of Platform - Enterprise Analytics, SAP

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Equipment Partner Functions, A_Equipment...
entry.content:
Hi Jerry, thanks for the API link - do you know in detail, if the filter for Partner on the EquipmentPartnerType is not implemented? Currently i cant filter for Partner (results in a empty result, i've tried with and without leading zeros). Thanks Alex

Edit: this note helped:
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003607432

wrong: "?$filter=Partner eq '12345678'"

correct: "?$filter=startswith(Partner, '12345678')"

entry.title: Re: CONFIGURE camt.053.001.08
entry.content: You requested document type AMT.053.001.08 for customer invoices. To my knowledge, this appears to be a Bank-to-Customer Statement ( ISO 20022, not a standard invoice type). Could you confirm if this is correct or if there was a misunderstanding?

entry.title: Evaluating Microsoft Entra for SAP SoD Analysis vs Saviynt
entry.content:
hello SMEs,

We are currently evaluating the feasibility of leveraging Microsoft Entra ID Governance / Entra for SAP SoD (Segregation of Duties) analysis as part of a potential migration away from Saviynt.

I wanted to understand from the community:

  • Has anyone implemented SAP SoD analysis using Entra?
  • How mature are the SAP governance capabilities compared to dedicated IGA platforms like Saviynt or SAP GRC?
  • Can Entra evaluate conflicts beyond the SAP Role level?
    • For example, does it support granular authorization object level analysis such as:
      • Transaction + authorization object combinations
      • Min/Max field value constraints
      • Activity value conflicts
      • Org-level restrictions (Company Code, Plant, Purchasing Org, etc.)
  • Or does the analysis primarily remain at a coarse-grained business role / entitlement level?

In Saviynt, we currently leverage fairly granular SoD rules that can evaluate down to authorization value depth within SAP roles. We are trying to assess whether similar depth is achievable natively with Entra, or whether this would require:

  • Custom integrations
  • SAP GRC coexistence
  • Defender / Sentinel correlation
  • Third-party governance tooling

Would appreciate hearing from anyone who has:

  • Implemented Entra for SAP IGA
  • Compared Entra vs Saviynt for SAP governance
  • Faced limitations around SAP authorization granularity
  • Used Entra with SAP ECC / S4HANA / Fiori landscapes

Thanks in advance for any insights or architectural guidance.

entry.title: Costs related to digital access resulting from integrations with non-SAP applications
entry.content: If I have third-party systems that I want to integrate with SAP S/4HANA Private Edition 2023, I know there are costs associated with Digital Access per document created. Can I save these costs by using an intermediate SAP HANA Cloud table in BTP for synchronous integrations, and for asynchronous integrations, using SFTP via unattended bots? Or could SAP still charge me for Digital Access during the audit?

entry.title: Re: SAP runs SAP BDC - Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data products
entry.content: Excited for this session! Looking forward to learning how SAP is operationalizing data products in SAP Business Data Cloud to drive AI-powered innovation and break down traditional IT bottlenecks. The focus on SAP-Managed Data Products and Intelligent Applications sounds especially valuable for organizations aiming to scale insights and automation effectively. Wishing the speaker all the best for the upcoming family event as well — and thank you to the team for ensuring the community can still benefit from the session!

entry.title: Re: Open QP Settlements data migration – Responsibility,...
entry.content:
Good day @valdir_junior453f80 

Thank you for your question.

I would not treat Open QP Settlements as a standard migration cockpit object unless a released object exists for your activate scope.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/886e1b9bf25245d093a98e0c635818e5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html?locale=en-US  

I suggest you check Migrate Your Data for a released migration object in your activated scope. If no object exists, avoid direct table-based migration and handle this as a cutover design topic.  

Settle as many QP items as possible before go-live, migrate only supported commercial or accounting open items where applicable, and keep an open QP cutover for manual or operational follow-up after go-live. If the business expects automated migration of the settlement status, I suggest you raise an incident to confirm whether a released object or supported API exists for your specific scope or release.

In Public Edition, the first check should be whether a released migration object exists in the Migrate Your Data app for this specific open settlement scenario. If no released migration object exists, it should be handled as a cutover design topic rather than as a direct migration object.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone Push Notifications not working on...
entry.content: I would recommend checking if push notifications are enabled for SAP Mobile Start on the device, also you might want to send test notifications via Push Registrations to confirm device capability

entry.title: Re: Converting Property Graphs to Knowledge Graphs in SAP HANA Cloud: A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Hi @shabana,

Does any advanced settings need to be enabled in order to work with Property Graph(PG), Knowledge Graph(KG) and as well as RDF?

I hope you will answer my query.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge - Week 1: Getting familiar with the events
entry.content:
{"specversion":"1.0","type":"sap.s4.custom.BusinessPartner.Created","source":"/sap/s4/erp/business-partner","id":"7b758ae4-5d7b-4ffe-9b22-7ca8735eb9dd","time":"2026-05-26T13:01:48.252Z","datacontenttype":"application/json","subject":"1003799","data":{"BusinessPartner":"1003799","BusinessPartnerUUID":"dcf8aa75-a6f4-4c1d-a84e-de77c558e865","BusinessPartnerFullName":"Allison King","BusinessPartnerCategory":"1","BusinessPartnerGrouping":"BP02","FirstName":"Allison","LastName":"King","IsNaturalPerson":"X","CreationDate":"/Date(1518393600000)/","CreatedByUser":"CC0000000002","BusinessPartnerAddress":{"Country":"CA","Region":"","CityName":"St-Onge Ouest","PostalCode":"V9B 7V3","StreetName":"0329 Christine Port","HouseNumber":"99914","AddressTimeZone":"EST"}}}

 

I have used Solace PubSub  as it is Available free trial and SAP AEM is powered by solace

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytic Cloud Usage Tracking Content will be replaced with new System Overview (Admin Conte
entry.content:
Hello @thomas_fery,

I got an email from SAP regarding usage of model SAP__SAC_USAGE_FILES in our tenant recently. I checked and found a story which uses date fields having a hierarchy on it including several fields which can be used as field in charts and tables:

The fields created, modified and last access don't have this option in successor models RESOURCE and RESOURCE_RECENT_ACCESS. We need this option in these models, too.

I created an influence item (375715) for it. Please have a look into it and implement it soon if possible. Due to the fact that the model SAP__SAC_USAGE_FILES will be phased out during upgrade to HANA of SAC tenant the topic is a bit urgent. 😉

Many thanks in advance!

Best regards

Lothar

P.S. @ All other members of the community: in case you miss this option, too, feel free to vote for the named influence item to give SAP the opportunity to see that this feature is really helpful. - Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: CONFIGURE camt.053.001.08
entry.content:
Dear @jerrysam,

Camt.053.001.08 is configured as an electronic bank statement format, not directly on the customer invoice. The customer invoice clearing is achieved only after the bank statement is imported and the system can match the bank statement item with the open customer item based on references such as invoice number, payment reference, End-to-End ID, structured remittance information, assignment or memo line

The correct configuration approach is:

1. Use configuration activity 170010 Map Format Data for Incoming Files from Banks. SAP provides the format mapping CAMT053_001_08, which can be used as the basis for bank files based on camt.053.001.08. If the bank file follows the expected ISO/CGI structure the standard mapping can be used. If the bank sends a bank-specific variant the standard mapping should be copied and adjusted only where required

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/5481c611480e4c23bf49e3af651d8e4d.html

2. Use configuration activity 101024 Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement to maintain account symbols and their G/L account assignment, transaction types, external transaction codes and the assignment of external transaction codes to SAP-delivered posting rules.

Important: The posting rules themselves are part of the SAP standard delivery and are not customer-configurable via SSCUI

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/828a6a386d0342319c64d22e24c62518.html

https://me.sap.com/notes/3357476

https://me.sap.com/notes/2854415

3. Use Manage Processing Rules For Bank Statements (F3555) to define how incoming payment items should be processed. The rules can be used for G/L and AP/AR account postings, including matching and clearing customer open items when the bank statement contains usable customer or invoice references

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/d6fe6526b26f46e59d9d1dd0f7427fe3.html

4. Use Manage Incoming Payment Files (F1680) to import the bank statement file. SAP confirms that this app is used to manually import bank statements and lockbox batches using electronic payment files, and SAP documentation lists CAMT.053, version 8 / CAMT.053.001.08 as a supported bank statement format

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1680')/S36

5. If the system cannot match or clear an item automatically the item should be reviewed in Reprocess Bank Statement Items (F1520). This is the standard operational follow-up when the bank statement item cannot be fully processed by the automatic rules

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1520')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/bc1fe656fe590950e10000000a44147b.html

Important: Automatic clearing will work only as well as the references provided in the bank file. If the CAMT file contains a reliable invoice number, payment reference, End-to-End ID or structured remittance reference the matching rule can be designed accordingly. If the bank sends only generic free text the item may still need manual reprocessing

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Innovation Day Berlin, Germany
entry.content: Is this in German? And only for DSAG?

entry.title: Re: SAP Customizing Translation into multiple languages
entry.content:
Hi @MAlbr ,

If we understand you correctly, you want to translate entries in “all customizing tables” into 5 languages. Probably you don't want to translate the contents of all customizing tables. It’s more likely that you want to translate the contents of specific customizing tables only.

The externalization function enables customers to export texts for translation and import the translations later. The function is described here: Translating Texts Offline | SAP Help Portal

If you have created table entries with language key EN in customizing tables with original language DE, then you will need to follow the instructions in this topic to facilitate translation into DE: Overriding the Original Language of Customizing Tables for Translation | SAP Help Portal

For further questions, please reach out to our certified language consultancy partners.
They are listed at the top of page 2 of this document: Partner_List.pdf

Best regards,
Eugenio

entry.title: Re: Moving HUs to storage locations on which HUs of the same material already exist.
entry.content: Interesting requirement. Standard “Addition to Existing Stock” may not fully work here since the focus is on HU consolidation rather than material consolidation at bin level. This scenario usually requires custom putaway/bin determination logic in EWM to prioritize storage bins already containing the same material across different HUs. Curious to know if anyone has implemented this using BAdIs or custom storage type search strategies.

entry.title: Re: 1H2026 Release: A journey to Autonomous Payroll with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: Do we have to be on HANA for it ?

entry.title: Re: Moving HUs to storage locations on which HUs of the same material already exist.
Re: Setting up Document Grounding for Joule with Google Drive
entry.content:
Hi @paveltieff,

the purpose of the pipeline itself is for bulk ingestions which is the standard case for grounding use cases. Otherwise you would need to create one pipeline for each file to be uploaded. So, file level sharing is currently not supported. However, if you want ground a single file, you could easily crate one folder with one file and create an ingestion pipeline with that.

Best, David.

entry.title: Re: GL Role Restriction based on Company Code.
entry.content: Hi @ksiddiqui, what might be happening is that the 'Read, Value Help' and 'Value Help' access categories for 'Company Code Hierarchies' are set to 'Unrestricted', allowing access to all company codes. I kindly ask you to check this KBA for resolution (https://me.sap.com/notes/0003595981). Remember to set the restrictions to the company codes you don't want the user to access and to put the restriction on "Value Help" on the general tab as well. I hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Veera Venkata Satyanarayana, an Aspiring Mechanical Engineer Stu
entry.content: This is super cool. Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: The future of data is conversational and agentic AI — Are you ready?
entry.content:
Thanks for posting your thoughts, ASAGNORI.  I couldn't agree more with this "AI should support professionals, not fully replace human judgment and accountability."  I not only agree, but I also think this is important ethically - for humans to remain "in charge."

@ASAGNORI - Question on you point around business context - are you leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud?  That is one of the key strengths and differentiators of the solution as a business data fabric:  At the center of the data fabric is the knowledge core. While compute and storage manage how data is processed, the knowledge core defines what that data means to the business and ensures that context is consistently applied across the entire landscape.

entry.title: Annotation @UI.selectionVariant for quantity field
entry.content:
Hi.

currently I have to implement a List View. The requirement is to use default values for a couple of fields. One default value should be defined on a quantity field greater than 0.

I tried to use @ui.selectionVariant in my Metadata Extension like this:

@ui.selectionVariant: [{ qualifier: 'sVariant', filter: '    MaterialBaseUnit EQ ST 
                                                         AND MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit gt 1                                                           
                                                         AND Plant EQ 0020
                                                         AND ( StorageLocation EQ 0001 OR StorageLocation EQ 0002 ) ' } ]

For Plant, StorageLocation and MatreialBaseUnit everything works fine. Only for the quantity field MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit the '1' is not taken over in the filter default values. Only '>' is taken over.

Just to ensure:

  • MatreialBaseUnit is the unit for MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit (@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MaterialBaseUnit')
  • I already tried 'GT', 'EQ', '>'
  • MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit has the Annotation @Consumption.filter.selectionType: #INTERVAL

So from my perspective this issue seems to be related to the quantity field. 

Any Ideas?

Thanks,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: SAP runs SAP BDC - Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data products
entry.content: Thank you for the kind words and thoughtful insights! We’re looking forward to a great session and to continuing the conversation with the community!

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Great hands-on exercise for understanding how SAP’s Generative AI Hub orchestration service simplifies enterprise AI development. The ability to switch between multiple models using the same codebase, combined with document grounding and prompt templating, makes implementing scalable RAG solutions much more efficient. Especially valuable for building context-aware AI applications with secure and structured enterprise knowledge retrieval.

entry.title: Re: Creating a Hybrid CAP (Node.js) Profile with PostgreSQL on BTP from Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hello, do you have any update ?, becausse i got stuck on the same issue:

Please could you help how connecting to postgreSQL  db via Hybrid Profile.

entry.title: Re: Manufacturer Part Numbers (MPNs) in SAP MM
entry.content: I like your screen shots showing the Infor Record Supplier Part Number field EINA-IDNLF. One thing that I have done is included the MPN "Material column" MEPO1211GRID-EMATN in my ME21N Screen to bring in the Manufacturer Part Number from the Material Master. It is a different field than the Material Info Record's "Supplier Part Number".

entry.title: How to add attachment to the send request in PPF?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am working on a requirement.

When a PPF action (External Comm) is executed and sends an email out, we can attach any files to this email and it works without any problem. But attachments are not visible in the below grid.

My question is that; how can we add attachments to the Send Request during the PPF execution. So that, attachments will be visible in the ATTACHMENTS Grid?

Tip: COMPLETE_SEND method is only for recipient adding purpose as far as i understand.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Excellent introduction to AI agent development with CrewAI and SAP Generative AI Hub. The combination of structured agent roles, task orchestration, and enterprise-grade LLM integration demonstrates how intelligent agents can handle real-world, domain-specific workflows effectively. The Hamburg Social Welfare Case Manager example is a great use case showing how AI agents can move beyond simple chat interactions into decision support and personalized service recommendations.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: Really interesting progression from basic AI agents to tool-equipped, knowledge-grounded agents. Integrating CrewAI with SAP’s grounding capabilities demonstrates the true potential of agentic AI — enabling agents to retrieve real-time, verifiable information instead of relying only on pretrained knowledge. The Hamburg Social Welfare Case Manager scenario is a strong example of how RAG + AI agents can deliver more accurate, contextual, and trustworthy decision support in real-world enterprise and public sector applications.

entry.title: Re: Optional Task: Use Joule CDS Unit Test Generation
entry.content: Great bonus task to explore another powerful capability of Joule in ABAP development. CDS Test Generation can significantly accelerate unit test creation and improve validation of CDS logic with minimal manual effort. This is a valuable hands-on exercise for understanding how AI-assisted development can enhance code quality, testing efficiency, and developer productivity in modern ABAP environments.

entry.title: Re: Open QP Settlements data migration – Responsibility,...
entry.content: Hello Chris, Thank you very much for your detailed guidance and for taking the time to address my question! Your insights regarding the migration strategy for Open QP Settlements are extremely valuable. Checking the availability of a released migration object within the Migrate Your Data app for our activated scope will definitely be our first step. Treating this complex scenario as a cutover design topic—while maximizing pre-Go-Live settlements and planning for manual or operational follow-up for the remaining open QPs—makes total sense and aligns perfectly with the best practices for a safe transition. I truly appreciate your availability, the shared documentation links, and your excellent recommendations. They have given our team a clear direction on how to approach this business-critical topic. Best regards, Valdir Brito Júnior.

entry.title: ODQ_TQ_JOB automatically restarting after stopping - How to identify the source PC or scheduler?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP BW regarding the background job ODQ_TQ_JOB.

Whenever I stop/cancel the job from SM37, it automatically starts again within 2–30 seconds.

Job Details:

- Job Name: ODQ_TQ_JOB
- Program: ODQ_TQ_WORKER
- Related task in logs: RSPC_RECENT_RUN_CACHE

System keeps showing:
- Task RSPC_RECENT_RUN_CACHE will be restarted in 30 seconds.
- Internal scheduled.

What I observed:

- Even after manually stopping the job, SAP recreates and runs it automatically.
- It looks like the job is being triggered by:
- Process Chain,
- Event,
- Scheduler,
- or some BW/ODQ background mechanism.

My Requirement:

I want to identify:

1. Which process chain, scheduler, or event is restarting this job?
2. How can I trace the exact source?
3. What is the correct way to stop this job permanently (if required)?

Transactions checked:
- SM37
- SM36
But I am still unable to identify the exact trigger source.

Log Snapshot:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Can you please help me as soon as possible what i exactly need to do?

Thanks & Regards.

entry.title: Re: May 2026 Developer Challenge - Week 2: Connecting to the broker and consuming events
entry.content: Built a SAP CPI flow using the Advanced Event Mesh adapter to consume Business Partner CloudEvents from a Solace queue,

entry.title: Manually Rolling Kernel update with ASCS/ERS in between (not as the first components)
entry.content:
Hello,

For one of our high availability systems, existing of ASCS/ERS including in a Pacemaker Cluster, splitter over two data center and several application servers.
When patching the OS we doing it in the below sequence to achieve downtime minimization:

  1. deactivation of half of the  application server (DC 2)
  2. OS patching VMs of half of  application server (DC 2)
  3. deactivation of the other half of application server (DC 1)
  4. OS patching of VM of ERS instance (DC 2)
  5. Failover of ASCS (DC 1) to VM of ERS (DC 2)
  6. OS patching of ex-VM of ASCS instance (DC 1)
  7. OS patching VMs of the other half of application server (DC 1)

You see we are doing first half of application server, then ERS and ASCS and in the end the last half of application server.

We are now planning to include SAP Kernel update in this sequence, so first tasks would be to extract new SAP Kernel in global kernel directory under /sapmnt/<SID>/exe and then starting the above mentioned sequence.
Can you make a comment if this is supported by SAP.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Manually Rolling Kernel update with ASCS/ERS in betwe...
entry.content:
Hello,

in the SAP Online Help Description of the RKS Procedure  is described the recommended sequence.

The reason behind is that the ASCS instance contains both the message server and the enqueue server. All other application server instances are connected with the ASCS instance.
In this way, when the application servers starts with new kernel are connected to the ASCS instance already with the new kernel release.

kind regards,
Mercedes

entry.title: How to reuse or modularize JavaScript in SAP CPI?
entry.content:
Introduction

As described in the article Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO , there is a concept of splitting code into smaller modules by using multiple scripts within an ifFow. However, the explanation applies only to Groovy.

I am looking for a similar approach using JavaScript, which is also available in SAP CPI.

POC - additional function outside of 'processData'

As a first step, I prepared POC using a single script. It contains the main function (processData) and an additional helper function defined outside of it. Script below : 

This approach works correctly. After testing via Postman, i received the expected results. 

POC - additional code as a seperate script (failure result)

In the next step, I tried to move the helper logic into separate script to improve readability and maintainability.. So i copied the code into a new script called "JS_ExtScript". Then in my main script "JS_withExtScript", I attempted to use the "importClass" syntax with the path src.main.resources.script.<script_name>.<class_name> .

However, the tests ended with an error . 

 

I did some research and found the following resources: 

- Try use JavaScript scripting instead of Groovy scripting in your CPI project , where someone from SAP recommended to check functions like defineClass, load, loadClass, but none of these worked in my case. 

- SAP Integration Suites javascript load library, similar question regarding my topic was raised, but no clear solution was provided. The author ended up keepin all logic in a single script, similar to my initial POC which works fine but this is not what i am looking for.

 

Has anyone successfully implemented modular JavaScript in SAP CPI (e.g. using multiple scripts or reusable libraries)?
Any suggestions or workarounds would be greatly appreciated.

Looking for you help. Thanks in advance !

entry.title: Re: Converting Property Graphs to Knowledge Graphs in SAP HANA Cloud: A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Hi @krishnam_prasanth - thanks for your question.

You will have to enable 'Triple Store' service on HANA Cloud Central tool (during or after HC instance provisioning) to work with Knowledge Graph Engine. Property Graph Engine does not require any such explicit service enablement. You can find more details here.

Thanks,

Shabana

entry.title: Re: Moving HUs to storage locations on which HUs of the same material already exist.
entry.content:
Are you sure you really mean Storage Location as a logistics organization level?

EWM doesn't really manages storage locations directly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas- The flavor ID provided in the UR...
entry.content: Hi Hoznek, Afternoon Thank you for your comments we are facing in the Script side //OPEN TST1 FLAVOR BY ID //session.utils.changeFlavor("758611FB04901FE192C3BFA324659EA7"); The previous flavor already exist in the Flavors Menu. I was trying to fin any SAP note for this I couldn't find anything about it

entry.title: How to import a Groovy class reference in Resources->References in CPI?
entry.content:
As shown in Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO | SAP Blogs classes that are in other scripts are available by importing them with:

import src.main.resources.script.ClassName

They just sitting in:

 

What happens when I put such a class in a separate package? What is the import path then?

 

I tried to move the class directly to a file in Common Script Collection and the import doesn't work. When the class is in the Local file the import works.

entry.title: Travel Management (PR05) - Mass Update Trip Status
entry.content:
Hello,

In light of the changes to the HMRC approved mileage rate from 6th April 2026 whereby this will increase from 45p per mile to 55p per mile for the first 10,000 miles, due to the size of our payroll, we have circa. 900 individual mileage claims that have already been processed since April 2026 that will now need to be re-processed retrospectively so that they receive the revised rate. 

I am aware of transaction PRAP to approve claims and PREC to settle claims but I am not aware of any transaction or programs that can be run for mass data to amend the trip status. Is there such a thing?

In order to re-process the 900 claims we need to change the trip status back to being "Trip Completed" so that they can be re-approved and re-settled so that they receive the uplifted rate. We are trying to identify if there is a way of doing this without the need for us to do this manually via PR05 900 times. 

Thanks
Emma

entry.title: Re: You Asked for Simpler. SAP Signavio Listened. Here Is What Changes.
entry.content: Process Insights provided the capability to limit what users can see security wise (certain company codes, sales org, purchase org). Where is that functionality in the new user interface?

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to mass generate order confirmations?
entry.content: If your query is answered, close the discussion by clicking "Accept as Solution".

entry.title: For restricting edit/create options for Ariba purchasing doc type in S4
entry.content:
We have Ariba PRs and POs with document type 'ARB' that gets copied in the S/4HANA. There is a requirement to restrict the creation and modification of Ariba-generated PRs and POs in S/4HANA. Most of the standard Fiori apps used for creation or changes of PRs/ POs have been restricted by the security team using authorization objects - M_BEST_BSA ; however, for the remaining four applications (listed below), the security team is unable to enforce restrictions due to the lack of authorization control at the document type level. Could you please suggest what can be done for the below apps so that changes to the document type 'ZAR' should be restricted.

App nameApp ID
Mass Changes to Purchase RequisitionsF2594
Mass Changes to Purchase OrdersF2593
Monitor Mass Changes – Purchase RequisitionsF4391
Monitor Mass Changes- Purchase orderF3332

entry.title: Re: LT10 single transfer order for multiple handling unit...
entry.content: Hello Dominik, thanks for your answer, i made a class using L_TO_CREATE_MULTIPLE and it seems to work, all that's left is to test the program with the data in dev

entry.title: Re: tax data is missing
entry.content:
Hi @SAPB1JIMI ,

Refer to SAP Note 3453851 and verify if it matches with your scenario.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as Fro...
entry.content:
Hi @Krishna42 ,

Check if it helps:

If you want to show your actual parameter names instead of "From Date / To Date":
  1. Open the report in SAP Crystal Reports Designer
  2. Right-click Parameter Fields → Select your date parameter → Edit
  3. In the Value Options section:
    • Set Allow range values = False
    • Set Allow discrete values = True
This will show your original parameter name with a single date picker.
 
Kind regards,
ANKIT CHAUHAN
SAP Support

entry.title: Mastering SAP Business Data Cloud - Discovery workshop 2026-Q2
entry.content:
Certified in SAP Business Data Cloud?  Now learn how to bring it to life with customers.

The “Mastering SAP Business Data Cloud – Discovery Workshop 2026-Q2” is designed for SAP Partner sales and presales teams ready to sharpen discovery conversations and lead with business value.

Two days.  Real workshop simulations.  Customer scenarios.  Architecture discussions. Group presentations.  Live coaching.

No long theory sessions.  No passive listening.

You’ll practice how to:
• Lead value advisory conversations
• Position SAP Business Data Cloud with confidence
• Guide customer discovery workshops
• Connect business challenges to solution outcomes

Plus, the multiplier model helps you scale these skills across your teams and customer engagements.

If you want your next customer meeting to feel more strategic, more engaging, and far less transactional, this workshop is for you.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014170.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessDataCloud #Presales #DataDriven #PartnerSuccess

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hello Dolores,

what a great article we got here from you !

Thank you for sharing!

Kind Regards,

Weihua

entry.title: Re: Approval Template issue
entry.content:
Hi @pmkpradeep ,

Check if the following queries return the results:

  • SELECT T0.DocEntry AS DraftKey, T0.WddStatus AS Draft_WddStatus, T1.Status AS OWDD_Status, T1.ProcesStat AS OWDD_ProcesStat, T1.DocEntry AS Final_DocEntry FROM ODRF T0 LEFT JOIN OWDD T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DraftEntry WHERE T0.WddStatus = 'Y' -- Draft shows approved AND (T1.ProcesStat <> 'Y' OR T1.Status <> 'Y')


  • SELECT DocEntry AS DraftKey, ObjType, WddStatus FROM ODRF WHERE WddStatus IN ('Y', 'N', 'W')  AND DocEntry NOT IN (SELECT DraftEntry FROM OWDD)

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas- The flavor ID provided in the UR...
entry.content: There is no known problem with this functionality. If your system is fully up to date with all notes properly implemented according to the Health Check, but the flavor assignment is still not working, then create a support incident.

entry.title: Joule Everywhere: Breaking Down SAP’s Biggest AI Announcements from Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
A quick recap of the Joule portfolio

Before Sapphire, there were already five distinct Joule offerings. A lot of people conflate them, so here's the breakdown:

Offering What it is Who it's for How it's delivered
Joule BaseThe base AI experience across SAP — natural language assistants embedded in appsAll SAP business usersIncluded with SAP Cloud subscriptions
Joule AgentsAI agents for automating multi-step business workflows end-to-endAll SAP business usersJoule Premium PUPM package
Joule StudioNo-code / low-code environment to build and manage custom AI agentsIT teams, partners, CoEsSAP Build capability
SAP Joule for DevelopersEmbedded AI for SAP Build and ABAP — code generation, testing, optimizationPro-code and low-code SAP developersJoule Premium PUPM package
SAP Joule for ConsultantsConversational AI guidance for SAP projects and cloud transformationsSystem integrators and IT teams inside SAP customersJoule Premium PUPM package

The key thing to understand: these aren't just tiers of the same thing. They're designed for completely different buyers and use cases. Joule Base is about making every SAP user faster. Joule Agents and Studio are about replacing entire workflows. The developer and consultant products are about accelerating delivery.

That structure hasn't gone away — Sapphire doubled down on it. But two of these pillars just got significantly extended.

What is Joule Work ?

Joule Work is a brand-new interface — a configurable workspace available on desktop, web, and mobile — that's designed to eventually replace traditional SAP app navigation.

Instead of navigating to a Fiori tile, opening a transaction, and clicking through a form, you describe what you want to accomplish. Joule figures out which agents, workflows, approvals, and data are needed, and executes on your behalf.

 

"The user becomes a different paradigm for how people engage with SAP software. They become the controller of a process that orchestrates a fleet of assistants and agents that do the work for them."

In practice, Joule Work is:

  • A unified dashboard (not just a chat widget) from which you can prompt, fetch data, surface insights, and execute transactions
  • Cross-system — designed to work across SAP and non-SAP environments in heterogenous landscapes
  • Voice-enabled — a LiveKit partnership adds voice capabilities (Early Adopter Care now, GA H2 2026)
  • Backed by the SAP Knowledge Graph — which encodes 50 years of SAP ERP semantics into machine-readable relationships, helping Joule navigate S/4HANA, Ariba, and SuccessFactors accurately even for entities it hasn't been explicitly trained on

Availability today: Joule Work mobile app is GA now. Desktop app and bidirectional A2A (agent-to-agent) capabilities are planned for Q4 2026.

My honest take: Joule Work is SAP's answer to the "Zero UI" future — enterprise software that operates through intent rather than navigation. Whether your users are ready for that shift is a separate conversation, but this is clearly where SAP is heading. The fact that Joule Work is interoperable across non-SAP systems is significant — this isn't just a prettier front-end for S/4HANA, it's intended to be the single operational surface for the whole enterprise landscape.

Joule Studio is now effectively Studio 2.0 — here's what changed

if you've been watching Joule Studio since it launched as a no-code/low-code build environment for custom AI, the Sapphire version is a substantial upgrade. SAP's CTO Philipp Herzig presented it in the keynote as the place where customers and partners can identify, design, and build agents for specific business outcomes.

The live demo showed a process consulting agent identifying a pricing and purchasing issue with an estimated ~$24M margin impact, then proposing a sales pricing validation agent as the solution. Studio then generated a product requirements document, technical specs, workflow logic, evaluations — and orchestrated the resulting agents together.

That's a long way from drag-and-drop no-code.

What's new in Studio

New developer tools and framework support:

  • Pro-code support for LangChain, Pydantic AI, and LlamaIndex
  • Developers can extend Studio-generated solutions in VS Code, Cursor, and other agentic IDEs they already use
  • Embedded n8n environment for visual multi-agent orchestration

New ecosystem partnerships:

  • Vercel — lets teams build fast, consumer-grade custom frontends (Next.js etc.) for their AI agents while keeping SAP enterprise controls intact. Sits alongside UI5 and Fiori as an option, not a replacement.
  • n8n — embedded visual workflow builder for orchestrating complex multi-agent scenarios
  • NVIDIA OpenShell — secure edge inference runtime (sub-second quality inspection and anomaly detection use cases via the NVIDIA partnership)

Model choice: Studio 2.0 is model-agnostic. LLMs from Anthropic, Cohere, Mistral AI, and others are available as the reasoning layer. You're not locked into one model vendor.

Free access: SAP announced free design-time access to Joule Studio — including AI-assisted development capabilities under fair-use limits — through the end of 2026. First production customers start onboarding in June 2026.

My honest take: The Vercel and n8n partnerships are the most interesting signal here. SAP is acknowledging that their developer ecosystem has expanded well beyond the traditional SAP developer profile. The ability to build with Next.js on top of SAP live data, or to orchestrate visually in n8n, lowers the barrier for a whole class of builders who weren't previously in the SAP orbit. That's a smart move.

The broader picture: Autonomous Suite and Business AI Platform

Joule Work and Studio didn't land in a vacuum. They're part of a wider architectural shift SAP announced at Sapphire:

SAP Business AI Platform: BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation have been consolidated into a single unified layer. The SAP Knowledge Graph sits at the core — a semantic representation of data structures, APIs, and database tables that all Joule capabilities draw on.

SAP Autonomous Suite: 200+ specialized agents and 50+ Joule Assistants embedded across Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, and CX. Some examples:

  • Autonomous Close Assistant — handles journal entries, reconciliations, and error resolution; targets compressing month-end close from weeks to days
  • Autonomous HCM — automates payroll, recruiting, workforce planning, upskilling, and org modeling across the full employee lifecycle
  • H&M demoed Store Intelligence Agents monitoring real-time retail data and an AI-powered InStore Concierge delivering personalized product recommendations

RPT-1 v1.5 (tabular foundation model): SAP's tabular model got upgraded with retrieval-augmented prediction (RAP) for datasets of any size, explainability features, and multi-modal orchestration across Gemini, OpenAI, AWS, and other vendors.

What should you actually do with this information?

If you're an SAP customer:

  • Sign up for free Joule Studio design-time access before the end of 2026. There's genuinely no reason not to start exploring.
  • Identify one high-volume, manual, rules-based process in your landscape that an agent could handle — Autonomous Close, headcount fill, purchase requisition approval — and scope an MVP.
  • Start thinking about agent governance now. When agents proliferate (and they will), you'll want a playbook for oversight, audit trails, and role-based controls before you're scrambling.

If you're an SAP partner or SI:

  • The traditional model of teaching users to navigate screens is being disrupted. Where your value shifts next is toward designing intent libraries, agent behaviours by role, and governance playbooks for your clients.
  • Build something on Joule Studio while access is free. The partner agent ecosystem is already active (680+ submissions in the prior round), and SAP TechEd will be the next showcase.

If you're an SAP developer:

  • The LangChain/Pydantic AI/LlamaIndex support means your existing Python AI skills are now first-class in the SAP ecosystem.
  • VS Code and Cursor integration removes the "I have to learn a new IDE" objection.
  • ABAP developers: the embedded AI capabilities for SAP Build and ABAP in Joule for Developers are the track to watch for your role specifically.

Final thought

SAP Sapphire 2026 ends the era as we've known it — software that records what people do — and begins something genuinely different. Whether every customer is ready to run at that vision today is another question. But the product surface is here, the architecture is consolidated, and the free on-ramp (Joule Studio through end of 2026) has no real excuse not to be explored.

Curious what others in the community are thinking — especially around agent governance and the Joule Work adoption curve.

entry.title: Restrict Posting of Both Cost Center & WBS Element Together in FI Supplier Invoice/Down Payment Pos
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We would like to understand whether the below behavior is standard in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and if there is any available configuration or validation to restrict it.

Currently, during FI postings such as:

  • FI Supplier invoices (KR document type)
  • Vendor/Supplier down payments

the system allows users to enter both a Cost Center and a WBS Element together in the same expense line item.

However, the business requirement is to allow only one cost object at a time for expense postings. For example:

  • If the user enters a Cost Center first, the system should not allow entry of a WBS Element.
  • Similarly, if a WBS Element is entered first, the system should restrict entry of a Cost Center.

Our understanding is that allowing both cost objects together is standard SAP functionality. We would like to know:

  1. Is this behavior confirmed as standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Is there any SSCUI, configuration, BAdI, validation, or standard workaround available to restrict users from entering both Cost Center and WBS Element together in the same line item?
  3. Has anyone implemented a similar validation requirement for FI supplier invoice or down payment postings?

Any guidance or best practice suggestions would be appreciated.

Thank you.

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: Re: ODQ_TQ_JOB automatically restarting after stopping -...
entry.content: Have you already read KBA 2892377 - Information about ODQ_TQ_JOB ?

entry.title: Cost Center Automatically Populated in Service Entry Sheet Despite Only WBS Element Being Entered
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud related to Service Entry Sheet (SES) postings and would like to understand the source of automatic Cost Center determination.

Scenario:

  • During Service Entry Sheet posting, the user is entering only a WBS Element.
  • No Cost Center is manually entered by the user.
  • However, the system is automatically populating a Cost Center in the SES line item.

We tried analyzing the source of this Cost Center and checked the following:

  1. The Cost Center assigned to the WBS Element used in the posting.
    • The Cost Center maintained in the WBS Element is different from the Cost Center appearing in the SES posting.
  2. OKB9 default account assignment.
    • We checked whether the G/L account used in the SES has any Cost Center maintained in OKB9.
    • No matching Cost Center assignment was found there as well.

Since the Cost Center appearing in the SES does not match either:

  • the WBS master data assignment, or
  • the OKB9 default assignment,

we are unable to identify from where exactly the system is deriving this Cost Center.

We would like to understand:

  1. What are the other possible sources or determination logic for automatic Cost Center derivation in SES postings?
  2. Are there any standard tables, derivation rules, account assignment settings, substitution logic, or integration points that should be checked?
  3. Has anyone faced a similar issue where the system derives a different Cost Center automatically during SES posting?

Any guidance on additional checks or troubleshooting steps would be highly appreciated.

Thank you.

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: Re: Curriculum Status direct link
entry.content: https://<company-domain>.plateau.com/learning/user/learning/viewCurriculumStatus.do

entry.title: Re: Shape What’s Next: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026 Is Now Open
entry.content: @Egbert_de_Jong : Yes, you need to log on with your S-User. You can find more info  here.

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI2025/2027 Support for Apache To...
entry.content: As of now, Tomcat 10 is not planned. We plan to move to latest Tomcat - Tomcat 11.X

entry.title: Re: You Asked for Simpler. SAP Signavio Listened. Here Is What Changes.
entry.content:
Hi @Monsasa ,

I was expecting the seamless integration of SAP Signavio Process Insights into the new UX. But unfortunately, I cannot find.
